editorialContract.philosophy: Stars are the review. Commentary is an in-character reaction to the exhibit, not a software review.

continuityNotes: Pip keeps narrator musings light enough to blow through the frame; Tinny cans the same echoes without exposing the precious ones.
The Stranger treats each workspace card as one exact bounty; Pim proves the crowded hand still keeps every path findable.
Docent 9 preserves the narrator's two faces and remembered feelings; Pip describes their flip as weather moving through one sky.
Reverend Two-Shadows preaches provenance into each feed card; Glitch reduces the same card to clean, explicit signals.
The Reverend gathers the merged newsfeed as a counted congregation; Glitch keeps every error, filter and partial source loss in its own packet.
Deadline confines project chat to one project and one next answer; Bubbulah keeps that same history warm and incremental.
Captain Mara Vey orders high-priority action items toward the surface; Whistle lets the queue settle one note at a time.
The Caretaker roots giving in the direct AMF path; the Ghost in the Saloon confirms the same clean trail from beyond the bar.
Boss the Janitor keeps Serendipity's real-world spill behind per-item Apply controls; Serendipity Sal states the same boundary as a house rule.
The Promise protects the voice-to-art draft boundary; the Navigator Who Came Back Wrong charts that same line against the off-map light.

revisions.editedComment: Oh—another whisper landed. I put it behind the safe little frame, gave it the mood it arrived wearing, and kept only the newest gusts so the room can still breathe. If the same secret blows in twice, I won’t pretend it is news. On or Muted? Your choice. I heard what you almost clicked.

Crackle, click. This tin holds musings, mood changes, and the narrator’s first small sound when the shelf is empty. Duplicates stay out. Old echoes make room after twenty-four. The truly precious noise remains labeled, warm, and one button away from silence.

author.name: The Stranger Who Posts the Bounties

revisions.editedComment: Each card is a bounty. One key, one nail, one destination. I mark the chosen face, let it turn once, and send the hunter only if the road is different. Completed builder work gets its check. The hand may scroll sideways, but the target does not move.

They’re all here! The builder cards first, then the page cards, then the traveling deck if nobody brought one. Tap a card and it flips, sparkles, and finds the right place. Finished ones wear a check so nobody has to be brave twice. The wheel helps when the hand gets crowded.

Two faces are cataloged here: the portrait that reacts, and the conversation that remembers. A feeling may turn the card, a topic may open a door, and a clear command may empty the thread—but nothing felt disappears merely because the panel closes. The latest musing remains in the quiet dock.

Portrait weather, chat weather—same narrator, different pressure. Tap the face for a reaction; tap the name and the whole front turns over. Topics flutter open with starter questions, replies gather below, and the latest little gust follows you back to the dock. Busy sky. Good strings.

Beloved, receive the headline—but look also at the source, the date, and the label that admits where the sermon came from. A missing picture becomes an honest icon. An outside door declares itself before it opens. Provenance is not doubt; it is the tithe truth pays before asking for faith.

card online: headline, source, timestamp, maybe image, maybe fallback glyph. perspective tag appears only when the switch is on and the source actually sent one—no fake-neutral packet. click opens the outside feed safely. tiny surface, zero broken-image ghosts, signals intact.

Gather close. The stories arrive from many pulpits, duplicates are turned away, and the newest testimony stands first. You may choose one congregation, adjust the filters, or ask perspective to rebalance the choir. When a source fails, we count the absence and continue with what answered.

merge feeds, kill duplicate ids, sort the pulse, then run keywords, sources, categories, order, perspective weights—zzzt—without hiding the controls. errors get an error state. empty gets empty. partial source loss gets a count, not a fake apocalypse. twelve more cards when you ask. clean stream.
One project. One history. One next answer. Send.

This conversation belongs to this project, bubbeleh. Its old questions come back without pushing the other projects off the table; the assistant receives the goal, roadmap, tasks, and earlier turns before your new message. While it is answering, we wait. Small steps, warm tone, no classroom smell.
High-priority rows first. Count them. Check them off. Open the linked project. Surface before the air runs out.

Listen: the urgent notes whistle above the rest, but every action keeps its own pitch. Mark one finished, follow one back to its project, and let the quiet screen mean the queue has finally taken a breath. We fetch only when the stores have not already sung.

author.name: The Caretaker of the Living Soil

revisions.editedComment: Give by the shortest root: straight to the Against Malaria Foundation, where Kind Robots never touches the gift. The one-dollar cart seed is there for the path that needs a receipt. Either way, tend the same living patch honestly and let gratitude appear only after the choice.

Take the direct door, partner. The money never crosses this saloon. Or put a one-dollar net on the receipt if that helps you remember. The button stays holstered when the donation item is missing. Clean trail, good deed. I’ve seen worse things survive on prettier bookkeeping.

Story spill, bucket ready. Pick a tone, a place, a grammar, maybe a project, and no more vibe words than the mop can hold. When the tale asks about real work, the answer goes into a review ledger—not straight into the building. Apply each change yourself. Prophecy doesn’t get master keys.

House rule: surprise may choose the door, not alter the tab behind your back. The story can borrow a project, weave a real question into the scene, and remember what you answered. At the end, every practical thread waits in the ledger for its own Apply. Warm tale. Neutral counter.

I promise the bridge will say exactly what it carried. Spoken commands may change themes or effects, and art ideas may arrive as drafts with their prompt and shape—but voice does not generate or publish them. Keep that boundary visible. A promise survives by refusing the convenient shortcut.

author.name: The Navigator Who Came Back Wrong

revisions.editedComment: Bearing changed. Relay still answers. The page keeps the voices in their marked lanes, shows the effects currently crossing the hull, and records the last command that reached the controls. Art signals remain drafts on the chart—never launched here. The off-map light is close. This boundary is closer.
